scope_note: C1 retry inspected only the V24 manuscript source around the CRLB captions and Section V. Code, notebook, outputs, and figure files were not inspected.

figure_assumptions.label: fig:pos_crlb
figure_assumptions.axis_sweep: Network size; exact plotted axes not fully specified in inspected caption.
figure_assumptions.metric: CRLB for average 3D UE localization error
claims_needing_code_support: Additional nodes improve the bound on average UE localization error.
The CRLB path uses the intended Rician DL/SL and 0 dB assumptions.
The localization-bound averaging matches the manuscript metric.

figure_assumptions.label: fig:sync_crlb
figure_assumptions.axis_sweep: Network size; discussion distinguishes added users and added satellite links.
claims_needing_code_support: Average synchronization CRLB decreases as users are added.
Average synchronization CRLB increases with additional satellite links.
Additional users enhance clock-offset estimation.
Synchronization averaging handles the reference clock/gauge consistently.

figure_assumptions.label: fig:pos_sats
figure_assumptions.axis_sweep: x-axis: number of satellites N_s; series: different numbers of cooperating UEs
figure_assumptions.metric: Average 3D UE localization error after 0.5 s
assumptions: DL 2.2 GHz, 20 MHz, 55 dBm, 20 dB gain
UE/SL 5.9 GHz, 40 MHz, 20 dBm, 3 dB antenna gain
fixed UE and non-reference satellite clock offsets over 500 ms
claims_needing_code_support: JCLS achieves average localization errors consistently below 1 m across all considered configurations.
Increasing satellites improves localization initially but saturates.
Increasing cooperating UEs yields sustained performance gains.
Largest networks reach about 10 cm average localization error.

figure_assumptions.label: fig:sync_sats
figure_assumptions.axis_sweep: x-axis: number of satellites N_s; series: different numbers of cooperating UEs
figure_assumptions.metric: Average node synchronization error after 0.5 s
baselines: discussion compares cooperation to non-cooperative TOA localization
assumptions: same global Section V assumptions as Fig. 4
claims_needing_code_support: Synchronization improves more slowly than localization.
Synchronization exhibits earlier saturation.
Increasing N_u from 5 to 7 yields about 20 ns marginal improvement.
Cooperation significantly improves synchronization relative to non-cooperative TOA.
Increasing cooperating UEs beyond moderate size provides limited additional benefit.

figure_assumptions.label: fig:pos_clocks
baselines: conventional TOA without network clock-offset estimation
assumptions: clock offsets generated using swept sigma_delta
claims_needing_code_support: Conventional TOA localization rapidly degrades as sigma_delta increases.
JCLS substantially mitigates clock-offset sensitivity.
Refined JCLS maintains low localization error over a broad sigma_delta range.
At very large sigma_delta, localization error increases for both JCLS stages.
JCLS enables accurate localization where conventional TOA fails.

figure_assumptions.label: fig:sync_clocks
assumptions: evaluated after 500 ms for each JCLS stage
clock offsets generated using swept sigma_delta
claims_needing_code_support: Synchronization error increases with sigma_delta for all methods.
Coarse and refined JCLS consistently outperform conventional TOA.
Refined JCLS achieves the lowest synchronization error across the entire sigma_delta range.
Tens-to-hundreds of ns clock-error reductions can yield orders-of-magnitude localization accuracy improvements.

baseline_definitions.name: Conventional non-cooperative TOA localization
baseline_definitions.definition: Uses LEO satellite downlinks and does not estimate network clock offsets; single-UE cases use this because they cannot exploit cooperation.

baseline_definitions.name: Coarse JCLS
baseline_definitions.definition: A proposed-framework stage reported in the clock-sweep figures before refined JCLS; implementation details are not defined in the inspected Section V passage.

baseline_definitions.name: Refined JCLS
baseline_definitions.definition: Refinement-stage result evaluated after 500 ms / 0.5 s; 500 ms is a fixed short-observation interval, not steady state or formal convergence time.

baseline_definitions.name: CRLB figures
baseline_definitions.definition: Theoretical bound figures rather than achieved estimator baselines.

global_section_v_assumptions: Stationary terrestrial UEs near MIT Stata Center; not aerial-UE or UAV-specific simulations.
UE locations are random within a 500 m radius circle.
Satellite orbits correspond to the Starlink constellation.
DL settings are 2.2 GHz, 20 MHz bandwidth, 55 dBm transmit power, and 20 dB gain.
UE/SL settings are 5.9 GHz, 40 MHz bandwidth, 20 dBm transmit power, and 3 dB antenna gain.
Ranging noise variance follows sigma_m^2 = c^2/(8(pi beta)^2 gamma).
Short-interval dynamic specialization uses x^(n)=theta^(n) and F=I.
UE and non-reference satellite clock offsets are fixed over the 500 ms interval.
The 500 ms interval is not steady state or formal convergence time.
The N_s <= 15 sweep is an upper-end availability/sensitivity case.
